text: The surgeon inserts a reusable transanal D-port into the anus, followed by the introduction of a 30-degree, 10-millimeter scope. A monocurve reusable grasping forceps is positioned at the nine o'clock port orifice, while a graduated straight grasping forceps is placed at the three o'clock port orifice. The degenerated rectal polyp is visible less than two centimeters from the dentic line. A monocurve reusable coagulating hook is inserted at the three o'clock port orifice, and safe margins around the lesion are marked circumferentially using the hook.

The surgeon begins the resection at the six o'clock position using monocurved grasping forceps and a monocurved coagulating hook. The mucosal and submucosal layers are incised, followed by the muscular layers, exposing the recto-rectal space. Dissection proceeds to the left lateral side, clearly visualizing the correct plane of dissection, and continues by incising the remnant rectal wall. The dissection reaches the upper part and is continued with the coagulating hook. The procedure maintains satisfactory ergonomics, with no hand conflicts. The dissection then starts on the right lateral side using the full thickness method, clearly showing the perirectal fatty tissue, rectal muscular layer, and rectal submucosal and mucosal layers.

The surgeon removes the completely freed specimen through the D-port for pathological evaluation. A monocled reusable needle holder is used to insert an absorbable barbed suture at the three o'clock port orifice. The rectal wall closure begins at the central point, proceeding to the left lateral side, with full thickness bites placed on the dentate line, ensuring the suture remains under tension.

The surgeon continues suturing the rectal wall, reaching the middle segment of the left lateral side and taking full thickness bites. Monocurve reusable scissors are inserted at the three o'clock port orifice to cut the first suture. A new absorbable barbed suture is started at the apex of the right lateral dissection, and the rectal wall closure is resumed using the full thickness method.

The surgeon places subsequent suture bites, reaching the central point where the previous suture began. An intercorporeal knot is created, and the final view of the cavity reveals the complete suture line with no visible flaps opening.
